# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
class Admin::ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :require_admin!

  # GET /admin/reports
  def index
    @saved_reports = SavedReport.order(created_at: :desc)
  end

  # POST /admin/reports/execute
  def execute
    @saved_report = SavedReport.find(params[:report_id])

    # User supplies parameter values, NOT raw SQL
    filter_params = report_params.fetch(:filters, {}).permit!.to_h

    @results = ReportExecutor.new(@saved_report, filter_params).execute
    @columns = @results.columns
    @rows = @results.rows

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render :results }
      format.csv { send_data generate_csv(@columns, @rows), filename: "report-#{Time.current.to_i}.csv" }
    end
  rescue ReportExecutor::InvalidFilterError => e
    flash[:alert] = "Invalid filter: #{e.message}"
    redirect_to admin_reports_path
  rescue ReportExecutor::QueryError => e
    flash[:alert] = "Report error: #{e.message}"
    redirect_to admin_reports_path
  end

  # POST /admin/reports (create a new saved report - super_admin only)
  def create
    authorize_super_admin!

    @saved_report = SavedReport.new(saved_report_params)

    if ReportValidator.valid?(@saved_report.query_template)
      @saved_report.save!
      flash[:notice] = "Report saved successfully."
      redirect_to admin_reports_path
    else
      flash[:alert] = "Report query failed validation. Only SELECT queries on allowed tables are permitted."
      render :new
    end
  end

  private

  def require_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      flash[:alert] = "Access denied."
      redirect_to root_path
    end
  end

  def authorize_super_admin!
    unless current_user&.super_admin?
      flash[:alert] = "Only super admins can create report templates."
      redirect_to admin_reports_path
    end
  end

  def report_params
    params.require(:report).permit(:report_id, filters: {})
  end

  def saved_report_params
    params.require(:saved_report).permit(:name, :description, :query_template, allowed_filters: [])
  end

  def generate_csv(columns, rows)
    require "csv"
    CSV.generate do |csv|
      csv << columns
      rows.each { |row| csv << row }
    end
  end
end

# app/models/saved_report.rb
class SavedReport < ApplicationRecord
  # columns: name, description, query_template (text), allowed_filters (jsonb), created_by_id
  validates :name, presence: true
  validates :query_template, presence: true
  validate :query_template_is_safe

  belongs_to :created_by, class_name: "User"

  private

  def query_template_is_safe
    unless ReportValidator.valid?(query_template)
      errors.add(:query_template, "contains disallowed SQL operations")
    end
  end
end

# app/services/report_validator.rb
class ReportValidator
  ALLOWED_TABLES = %w[
    orders
    products
    order_items
    categories
    page_views
    analytics_events
    report_aggregates
  ].freeze

  DISALLOWED_KEYWORDS = %w[
    INSERT UPDATE DELETE DROP ALTER CREATE TRUNCATE EXEC EXECUTE
    GRANT REVOKE INTO MERGE REPLACE SET CALL
    pg_read_file pg_ls_dir pg_stat_file
    LOAD_FILE OUTFILE DUMPFILE
    xp_ sp_
  ].freeze

  def self.valid?(query_template)
    return false if query_template.blank?

    normalized = query_template.strip.gsub(/\s+/, " ").upcase

    # Must start with SELECT or WITH (for CTEs)
    return false unless normalized.match?(/\A\s*(SELECT|WITH)\s/i)

    # Must not contain dangerous keywords
    DISALLOWED_KEYWORDS.each do |keyword|
      return false if normalized.include?(keyword.upcase)
    end

    # Must not contain multiple statements
    # Remove strings first to avoid false positives on semicolons in strings
    without_strings = query_template.gsub(/'[^']*'/, "").gsub(/"[^"]*"/, "")
    return false if without_strings.include?(";")

    # Must not contain comments (could hide malicious SQL)
    return false if query_template.match?(%r{--|/\*|\*/})

    # Validate only allowed tables are referenced (basic check)
    # Extract table names from FROM and JOIN clauses
    table_references = query_template.scan(/(?:FROM|JOIN)\s+(\w+)/i).flatten.map(&:downcase)
    table_references.each do |table|
      return false unless ALLOWED_TABLES.include?(table)
    end

    # Try EXPLAIN to validate syntax without executing
    begin
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("EXPLAIN #{query_template.gsub(/:\w+/, "'test'")}")
    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid
      return false
    end

    true
  end
end

# app/services/report_executor.rb
class ReportExecutor
  class InvalidFilterError < StandardError; end
  class QueryError < StandardError; end

  STATEMENT_TIMEOUT = "SET LOCAL statement_timeout = '30s'"
  ROW_LIMIT = 10_000

  def initialize(saved_report, filter_values = {})
    @saved_report = saved_report
    @filter_values = filter_values
  end

  def execute
    validate_filters!

    query = build_parameterized_query
    sanitized_query = apply_row_limit(query)

    execute_safely(sanitized_query)
  rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
    Rails.logger.error("Report execution failed: #{e.message}")
    raise QueryError, "Query execution failed. Please check your filters and try again."
  end

  private

  def validate_filters!
    allowed = @saved_report.allowed_filters || []

    @filter_values.each_key do |key|
      unless allowed.include?(key.to_s)
        raise InvalidFilterError, "Filter '#{key}' is not allowed for this report"
      end
    end

    # Validate filter value types (no SQL injection through values)
    @filter_values.each do |key, value|
      unless safe_value?(value)
        raise InvalidFilterError, "Filter value for '#{key}' contains invalid characters"
      end
    end
  end

  def safe_value?(value)
    # Allow strings, numbers, dates, arrays of simple values
    case value
    when Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass
      true
    when String
      # Reject strings with SQL-injection patterns
      !value.match?(%r{;|--|/\*|\*/|'.*'|".*"|\bOR\b.*=.*|UNION|SELECT}i)
    when Array
      value.all? { |v| safe_value?(v) }
    else
      false
    end
  end

  def build_parameterized_query
    query = @saved_report.query_template.dup

    # Replace named placeholders with properly sanitized values
    # Template uses :placeholder_name syntax
    @filter_values.each do |key, value|
      placeholder = ":#{key}"
      sanitized_value = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(value)
      query = query.gsub(placeholder, sanitized_value)
    end

    # Ensure no unresolved placeholders remain
    if query.match?(/:\w+/)
      unresolved = query.scan(/:\w+/).uniq
      raise InvalidFilterError, "Missing required filters: #{unresolved.join(', ')}"
    end

    query
  end

  def apply_row_limit(query)
    if query.match?(/LIMIT\s+\d+/i)
      # Replace existing limit if it exceeds our max
      query.gsub(/LIMIT\s+(\d+)/i) do
        limit = [Regexp.last_match(1).to_i, ROW_LIMIT].min
        "LIMIT #{limit}"
      end
    else
      "#{query} LIMIT #{ROW_LIMIT}"
    end
  end

  def execute_safely(query)
    # Use a read-only connection with timeout
    # Run inside a transaction that we'll roll back to prevent any writes
    result = nil

    ActiveRecord::Base.connection_pool.with_connection do |conn|
      conn.transaction do
        conn.execute(STATEMENT_TIMEOUT)

        # Set transaction to read only (PostgreSQL)
        conn.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY")

        result = conn.exec_query(query)

        # Always rollback - we only want to read
        raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
      end
    end

    # Re-execute outside the rolled-back transaction since result was lost
    ActiveRecord::Base.connection_pool.with_connection do |conn|
      conn.transaction do
        conn.execute(STATEMENT_TIMEOUT)
        conn.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY")
        result = conn.exec_query(query)
        raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
      end
    end

    AuditLog.create!(
      user: Current.user,
      action: "report_executed",
      details: {
        report_id: @saved_report.id,
        report_name: @saved_report.name,
        filters: @filter_values.keys,
        row_count: result.rows.size
      }
    )

    result
  end
end

# db/migrate/XXXXXX_create_saved_reports.rb
class CreateSavedReports < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    create_table :saved_reports do |t|
      t.string :name, null: false
      t.text :description
      t.text :query_template, null: false
      t.jsonb :allowed_filters, default: []
      t.references :created_by, null: false, foreign_key: { to_table: :users }
      t.timestamps
    end

    create_table :audit_logs do |t|
      t.references :user, null: false, foreign_key: true
      t.string :action, null: false
      t.jsonb :details, default: {}
      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :audit_logs, :action
    add_index :audit_logs, :created_at
  end
end

# config/routes.rb (partial)

#   resources :reports, only: [:index, :new, :create] do
#     collection do
#       post :execute
#     end
